exercises.title: Hello World
exercises.solutionCode:
using System;
class Program { static void Main() { Console.WriteLine("Hello, World!"); } }

exercises.title: Variables
exercises.description: Declare int age = 25 and string name = "CSharp". Print both.

exercises.title: If-Else
For Loop
exercises.description: Print numbers 1 to 5 using a for loop.
exercises.solutionCode:
using System;
class Program { static void Main() { for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++) Console.WriteLine(i); } }

exercises.title: Arrays
exercises.description: Create int[] nums = {10, 20, 30} and print each element.

exercises.title: Methods
exercises.description: Create a static method Greet() that prints Hello from C#!. Call it.
exercises.hint: static void Greet() { Console.WriteLine("Hello from C#!"); }

exercises.title: String Methods
exercises.description: Given string s = "Hello, CSharp!", print its length and uppercase version.

exercises.title: Classes
exercises.description: Create a Car class with Brand and Year. Instantiate with Toyota and 2024. Print them.
exercises.hint: public string Brand; public int Year; Use a constructor.

exercises.title: Lists
exercises.description: Create a List<string>, add Apple, Banana, Cherry. Remove Banana and print the list.
exercises.hint: List<string> fruits = new List<string>(); fruits.Add("Apple"); fruits.Remove("Banana");

exercises.title: Try-Catch
exercises.description: Write a try-catch that attempts to parse "abc" as int. Catch FormatException and print Invalid format!. Then parse "42" and print it.
